title: The Bacchae: Dionysus Arrives
description: Dionysus returns to Thebes disguised as a mortal; King Pentheus resists and is destroyed.
introduction: Euripides' final masterpiece stages the arrival of Dionysus — god of wine, ecstasy, and madness — in a city that refuses to acknowledge him. King Pentheus insists on rational control; Dionysus offers dissolution and transcendence. The play asks whether civilization can exist without acknowledging the irrational forces within human nature, and answers with devastating violence when Pentheus' own mother tears him apart in a Bacchic frenzy.

translation.original_text: ἥκω Διὸς παῖς τήνδε Θηβαίαν χθόνα Διόνυσος, ὃν τίκτει ποθ᾿ ἡ Κάδμου κόρη Σεμέλη λοχευθεῖσα κεραυνίῳ πυρί.
translation.text: I have come, the son of Zeus, to this land of Thebes — I, Dionysus, whom Cadmus' daughter Semele once bore, delivered by lightning fire.

translation.original_text: ὁ δαίμων ὁ Διὸς παῖς χαίρει μὲν θαλίαισιν, φιλεῖ δ᾿ ὀλβοδότειραν Εἰρήναν, κουροτρόφον θεάν.
translation.text: The god, the son of Zeus, delights in feasting, and loves Peace, the giver of prosperity, the goddess who nurtures the young.
